brief.speaker: grayson
brief.text: Workforce by borough. One row per borough with a count. Sorted so the largest borough is at the top. This is the chart they want to see.

expected.sql: SELECT borough, COUNT(*) FROM census_1890 GROUP BY borough ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC

hints.text: Grayson: 'Too many rows. Are you grouping correctly?'
Grayson: 'Right structure, wrong order or columns. Boroughs grouped, COUNT(*) ordered descending.'
Grayson: 'Every non-aggregate in SELECT must appear in GROUP BY.'
Grayson: 'SELECT borough, COUNT — GROUP BY borough — ORDER BY COUNT descending.'

success.speaker: grayson
success.text: Manhattan leads. Brooklyn second. Bronx, Queens, Staten Island trailing. And... there's a sixth row. A null borough. One resident. How? I do not sign off on data anomalies. Find out who that is.
